member.name: T:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier
member.summary: A model that is attached to a card to modify its behavior. Receives all combat hooks, and is capable of modifying the card's description. More features to be added in the future. TODO - in progress - base value modification like enchants/afflictions Passive cost modification? Without calling cost modification methods; works as a "modifier" in EnergyCost

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.Get``1
member.summary: Obtains a new instance of a CardModifier from ModelDb using .

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.StoreSaveData(BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.ModifierSave)
member.summary: Store values that must be saved in IntProperties or AdditionalProperties. Override this and if you need to save additional information besides .

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.LoadSaveData(BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.ModifierSave)
member.summary: Loads saved values into a new instance of this modifier. Override this and if you need to save additional information besides .

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.Modifiers(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.CardModel)
member.summary: Gets the list of modifiers on a card. This list is read-only and cannot be modified.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.DirectModifiers(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.CardModel)
member.summary: Gets the list of modifiers on a card. Modifying this list will affect the modifiers on the card. It is not recommended to modify this list directly.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.AddModifier``1(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.CardModel)
member.summary: Adds a card modifier to a card.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.AddModifier``1(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.CardModel,System.Int32)
member.summary: Adds a card modifier to a card with a specified amount.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.AddModifier(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.CardModel,BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier)
member.summary: Adds a card modifier to a card. The modifier being applied should be a mutable instance. Use the overload with a generic parameter if you don't need to set up the modifier before application. Otherwise, obtain a mutable instance using the ModelDb.CardModifier extension methods.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.RemoveModifier(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.CardModel,BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier)
member.summary: Remove a specific CardModifier instance from a card.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.ShouldReceiveCombatHooks
member.summary: Mostly unused; overridden just in case.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.Amount
member.summary: An integer value attached to enchantments that is saved.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.ApplyStacked(BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier)
member.summary: This method is called when a modifier is applied to a card that already has the same modifier. Return true to cancel the original application.
member.param: The modifier being applied.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.Priority
member.summary: Affects the ordering of card modifiers when they are added to a card. Lower priority means the card modifier will be inserted before card modifiers of higher priority.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.CanonicalVars
member.summary: Dynamic variables attached to each instance of the card modifier. Will automatically be attached to LocStrings retrieved using the method.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.GetLoc(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves a from a card_modifiers.json table using this modifier's ID. Adds this modifier's dynamic variables, , and attached card's TargetType to the loc.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.ModifyDescription(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Entities.Creatures.Creature,System.String@)
member.summary: Modifies a card's description before the game processes it. Receives target passed into CardModel.GetDescriptionForPile.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.ModifyDescriptionPost(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Entities.Creatures.Creature,System.String@)
member.summary: Modifies a card's description after the game processes it. Receives target passed into CardModel.GetDescriptionForPile.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.AddTips(System.Collections.Generic.List{MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.HoverTips.IHoverTip})
member.summary: Receives the card's list of tips to add to.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.OnInitialApplication
member.summary: Called after the modifier is applied to a card, including when a card is copied. Due to nature of when this occurs, async combat effects should not occur here.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.OnUpgrade
member.summary: Called after the card's OnUpgrade method is called.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.OnDowngrade
member.summary: Called after the card's OnDowngrade method is called.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.UpdateDynamicVarPreview(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Entities.Cards.CardPreviewMode,MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Entities.Creatures.Creature,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Called whenever the attached card updates its dynamic variable previews to update the modifier's dynamic vars. Can be overridden if some custom behavior to update display information is needed.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.AfterClonedOnCard(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.CardModel)
member.summary: Called after the card modifier is cloned and added to a clone of a card. Called whenever a MutableClone is created.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.ModifyBaseDamageAdditive(System.Decimal,MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.ValueProps.ValueProp)
member.summary: Functions like an EnchantmentModel's EnchantDamageAdditive. Add to the amount of damage that this modifier's card does. This hook runs BEFORE all other damage modification hooks. NOT YET FULLY FUNCTIONAL.
member.param: The amount of damage that would be dealt.
ValueProp for damage.
member.returns: Amount of damage to be added.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.ModifyBaseDamageMultiplicative(System.Decimal,MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.ValueProps.ValueProp)
member.summary: Functions like an EnchantmentModel's EnchantDamageMultiplicative. Multiply the amount of damage that this modifier's card does. This hook runs BEFORE all other damage modification hooks. NOT YET FULLY FUNCTIONAL.
member.param: The amount of damage that would be dealt.
ValueProp for damage.
member.returns: Amount that the damage should be multiplied by.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.ModifyBaseBlockAdditive(System.Decimal)
member.summary: Functions like an EnchantmentModel's EnchantBlockAdditive. Add to the amount of block that this modifier's card gains. This hook runs BEFORE all other block modification hooks. NOT YET FUNCTIONAL.
member.param: The original amount of block that would be gained.
member.returns: The amount to add to the block gain.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CardModifier.ModifyBaseBlockMultiplicative(System.Decimal)
member.summary: Functions like an EnchantmentModel's EnchantBlockMultiplicative. Modify the amount of block that this modifier's card gains. This hook runs BEFORE all other block modification hooks. NOT YET FUNCTIONAL.
member.param: The original amount of block that would be gained.
member.returns: The amount to multiply the block gain by.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomActModel.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.param: Set to -1 to prevent your act from spawning naturally. Otherwise, use 1/2/3 for the corresponding act.
If false, will not be added to CustomContentDictionary.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomActModel.GetUnlockedAncients(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Unlocks.UnlockState)
member.summary: By default, all ancients preset in the act are considered unlocked.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomActModel.AllAncients
member.summary: Default override is provided that returns ancients based on act number. If you don't want the default ancients or have a non-basegame act number, override this. If making custom ancients for your specific act, add them to the act using their IsValidForAct method, rather than by adding them to this method.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomActModel.BossDiscoveryOrder
member.summary: Fixed order in which bosses will first appear. Sets boss to first unseen encounter in provided set. Default override of empty list is provided.

member.summary: Required abstract method. Only used in Overgrowth specifically for the very first run, so default override is provided.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomActModel.BaseNumberOfRooms
member.summary: By default, Act 1 has 15 rooms, Act 2 has 14, and Act 3 has 13.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomActModel.GetMapPointTypes(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Random.Rng)
member.summary: Specifically sets rest and unknown room count. Set up to provide values matching basegame acts based on act number. (Lower numbers on higher acts due to lower room count)

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomActModel.CustomChestScene
member.summary:
Override this if you want to replace the chest visuals in Treasure Rooms.
The scene's root node must have a script attached that derives from or have no script at all.
If making custom visuals you will most likely want to make a custom script inheriting NCustomTreasureRoomChest to allow for reactivity to player actions.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomActModel.CustomGenerateBackgroundAssets(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Random.Rng)

Suggested to check act.ActNumber == 2 or 3.
If you are overriding ShouldForceSpawn, you should override this and return false.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomAncientModel.ShouldForceSpawn(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.ActModel,MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.AncientEventModel)
member.summary: Suggested to leave this set to false unless you want to force a specific ancient to spawn during map generation. Messing with this can cause mod conflicts, please only use if it is 100% necessary for your mod to function.
member.param: The ancient that will have been chosen by the games rng.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomAncientModel.MakeOptionPools
member.summary: Set up a new OptionPools with 1, 2, or 3 pools using MakePool for each pool. If there is 1 pool, all ancient options will be chosen randomly from this pool. With 2 pools, the first two options will use the first pool and the last option will use the second pool. With 3 pools, each option will use its own pool. You can give relics spawn conditions by calling AddCustomAncientSpawnCondition in your relic's constructor.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomAncientModel.AfterCloned
member.summary: Resets option pools to avoid shared field references.

Move props. Affects behavior of damage/calculation.

member.summary: Material is a shader material that will be applied to the frame texture. A custom frame can be used by overriding . Override this only if you have a custom shadermaterial. If not overridden, a custom material will be automatically defined using .

member.summary: Used for HSV values of material shader. HSV can instead be overridden directly.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomCharacterSelectEntry

member.summary: Creates and auto-registers the entry with BaseLib.

member.summary: Icon shown on the custom character select button.

member.summary: Title shown in the info panel while no concrete character is currently resolved.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomCharacterSelectEntry.SortOrder
member.summary: Sort order among custom entries. Lower values appear first.

member.summary: Optional character whose vanilla unlock state and lock tooltip semantics should be reused by this entry.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomCharacterSelectEntry.UnlockedInCharacterSelect
member.summary: Controls whether this entry is currently unlocked and can be selected. Defaults to the unlock state of when one is provided.

member.summary: Optional default resolved character when the entry is selected.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomEnchantmentModel.CustomIconPath
member.summary: Override this or place your enchantment's image at "res://images/enchantments/modid-enchantment_name.png"

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomEncounterModel.IsValidForAct(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.ActModel)
member.summary: Specifies where this encounter can appear. Generally encounters are act specific, so a check like "act is Glory" is recommended. If making a custom act, you are suggested to add your encounters to the act this way and leave the act's normal encounter list empty.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomEncounterModel.CustomScenePath
member.summary: The path to an encounter scene. An encounter scene is a 1920x1080 Control with Full Rect anchors and MouseFilter Ignore, with Marker2D children for enemy positions. The names of these markers can be used with CreatureCmd.Add when spawning additional enemies. Initial enemies will be placed at these markers in the order they exist in the scene. If using a custom scene the method must return the names of all the slots. A default implementation that reads the custom scene is provided, but it can be overridden.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomEncounterModel.HasScene
member.summary: Should not be necessary to override; will return true if CustomScenePath returns a valid resource path or a scene exists at the basegame expected path res://scenes/encounters/modname-encounter_name.tscn

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomEncounterModel.PrepCustomBackground(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.ActModel,MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Random.Rng)
member.summary: Generates and stores a custom background.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomEncounterModel.HasCustomBackground
member.summary: Works automatically if CustomEncounterBackground is overridden. If not using CustomEncounterBackground and instead placing files in basegame expected paths, override this to return true.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomEncounterModel.CustomEncounterBackground(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.ActModel,MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Random.Rng)
member.summary: Override this method if you want to provide a custom encounter background for your scene using custom paths. To do so, return a new CustomBackgroundAssets object. Alternatively you can place your assets at res://scenes/backgrounds/modname-encounter_name/layers and res://scenes/backgrounds/modname-encounter_name/modname-encounter_name_background.tscn, then override HasCustomBackground.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomEncounterModel.CustomRunHistoryIconPath
member.summary: See RoomIconPathPatch These are used as boss icons.

member.summary: The acts this event can spawn in. If none are set, the event will be considered a "shared" event and can spawn in any act. If you don't want the event to spawn, override IsAllowed. If making a custom act, set this rather than adding to the act model's event list.

member.summary: A message that when sent to host, will be passed on to other players.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.ICustomMessage.ShouldBuffer

member.summary: Method of message transfer. Override to Unreliable only for purely visual effects, such as communicating what a player is hovering.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomModifierModel
member.summary: Base class for custom run modifiers added by mods. Automatically registered into or based on .

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomModifierModel.Alignment
member.summary: Whether this modifier appears in the good or bad modifiers list.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomModifierModel.MutuallyExclusiveGroup
member.summary: Other modifiers that cannot be active at the same time as this one. Exclusivity is automatically symmetric — if A excludes B, ticking A unticks B and vice versa. Return empty if no exclusivity is needed.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomModifierModel.SortOrder
member.summary: Position within the good or bad modifiers list relative to vanilla modifiers. Negative values insert before vanilla modifiers, positive values insert after.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Abstracts.ModifierAlignment
member.summary: Determines whether a custom modifier appears in the good or bad modifiers list on the custom run screen.

member.name: F:BaseLib.Abstracts.ModifierAlignment.None
member.summary: The modifier does not appear in either list.

member.name: F:BaseLib.Abstracts.ModifierAlignment.Good
member.summary: The modifier appears in , shown as a beneficial run modifier on the custom run screen.

member.name: F:BaseLib.Abstracts.ModifierAlignment.Bad
member.summary: The modifier appears in , shown as a detrimental run modifier on the custom run screen.

member.summary: Appends custom good modifiers to .

member.summary: Appends custom bad modifiers to .

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.ModelDbPatches.CustomMutuallyExclusive
member.summary: Merged exclusivity groups derived from all custom modifiers' declarations. Uses union-find to merge transitively connected modifiers into single sets. Cached after first access.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.ModelDbPatches.MutuallyExclusiveModifiers(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlySet{MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.ModifierModel}})
member.summary: Merges custom exclusivity groups into , combining with existing vanilla sets when a custom group overlaps with them. This ensures both the daily run roller and the custom run UI respect custom exclusivity.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.CustomReward.GetLoc
member.summary: Recommended for use in the reward's Description method. If additional variables are needed for the localization, add them using locString.Add("Key", value).
member.returns: A LocString for the gameplay_ui table with the key MODID-REWARD_CLASS_NAME

members.member:
Other Overrides Populate - Prepares actual contents of rewards. Called if IsPopulated is false. IsPopulated - Return true if reward is ready. Should be true after Populate is successfully called, or by default if it's not necessary. ToSerializable - If the reward has special information that must be saved. Description IconPath
Set the reward index after vanilla rewards by default

member.name: T:BaseLib.Abstracts.IHasSecondAmount
member.summary: Exposes a secondary display amount alongside the primary power amount. Implement this interface on any PowerModel that needs to show a second numeric or textual value in the UI (e.g. a counter or cooldown).
member.remarks: When the value changes, call to trigger a UI refresh.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Abstracts.IHasSecondAmount.GetSecondAmount
member.summary: Gets the secondary amount as a formatted string for UI display.
member.returns: A string representing the secondary value.

summary.para: A model that implements this interface can define localization that will be added to its relevant localization table. Recommended implementation is to return one of the provided Loc classes such as CardLoc. To support translation, a switch statement like this is recommended:
summary.code: Localization => LocManager.Instance.Language switch { "aaa" => new CardLoc("translated"), _ => new CardLoc("default") };

member.name: T:BaseLib.Abstracts.ITomeCard
member.summary:
Marks a card as a target for Darv's Dusty Tome relic. If multiple cards are marked as tome cards for the same character, one will be chosen at random. If this interface is not used, a random non-archaic tooth ancient card is used by Dusty Tome.
Has a default method of checking character based on card pool, but can be explicitly set by implementing TomeCharacter.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Abstracts.ITomeCard.TomeCharacter
member.summary: The character that this is the Dusty Tome card for.

member.param: The sound to play
Adjustment to volume in dB
Multiplier on final volume
Random pitch (and speed) variation; range is centered on basePitch
Pitch (and speed) to play sound at. 2f is twice the pitch, 0.5f is half the pitch.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Audio.AutoModAudio
member.summary:
A class that allows playing of all audio files within a folder using shortened names. Given a file "res://ModMod/audio/boop.ogg" and an AutoModAudio initialized using the path "res://MyMod/audio", it can be played with PlaySfx("boop.ogg").
You are suggested to define a static variable in your main file with an instance of this class. Alternatively, you can define a static class with static ModSound variables to manually define your sounds.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Audio.AutoModAudio.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary:
A class that allows playing of all audio files within a folder using shortened names. Given a file "res://ModMod/audio/boop.ogg" and an AutoModAudio initialized using the path "res://MyMod/audio", it can be played with PlaySfx("boop.ogg").
You are suggested to define a static variable in your main file with an instance of this class. Alternatively, you can define a static class with static ModSound variables to manually define your sounds.

Pitch (and speed) to play sound at. 2f is twice the pitch, 0.5f is half the pitch.

member.name: T:BaseLib.BaseLibScenes.Acts.NCustomTreasureRoomChest
member.summary:
Scenes that are intended to replace the visuals of a chest in Treasure rooms through need to have a script attached to the root control which derives from this class.
Forwards the basic event handlers from the NTreasureRoom node:
OnChestButtonReleased used to start the open animation
OnMouseEntered used to show the highlight
OnMouseExited used to hide the highlight

members.member:
See PurgePatch and AfterCardPlayedPatch
A card that removes itself from the deck when played.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Cards.Variables.CustomCalculatedBlockVar
member.summary: A CalculatedBlockVar that allows a custom name and can have multiple on one model. Also works on relics and powers. Requires two additional vars with the same name ending in "Base" and "Extra". A CalculatedBlockVar named "Shield" would need "ShieldBase" and "ShieldExtra". WithMultiplier should be called and provide a multiplier calc using the variable's owner's type. A multiplier calc can be individually defined for cards, relics, and powers.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Cards.Variables.CustomCalculatedBlockVar.CalculateCustom(MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Entities.Creatures.Creature)
member.summary: Calculates the final value of this variable.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Cards.Variables.CustomCalculatedBlockVar.WithMultiplier(System.Func{MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.RelicModel,MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Entities.Creatures.Creature,System.Decimal})
member.summary: Sets a multiplier calculation for a relic. Note that calculation for relics is allowed to occur out of combat, so combat state may be null.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Cards.Variables.CustomCalculatedBlockVar.WithMultiplier(System.Func{MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Models.PowerModel,MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Entities.Creatures.Creature,System.Decimal})
member.summary: Sets a multiplier calculation for a power.

member.summary: Sets a calculation that supports any type that can be cast to . Note that calculation for relics and potions is allowed to occur out of combat, so combat state may be null.

member.summary: Opens a card selection screen where a specific number of cards must be selected from specified card piles and returns the selection result.
member.param: A predicate that takes a card and returns true
The card pile types from which the player can select cards. Shown cards will be sorted by pile type in the order you supply them.
member.returns: The selected cards

member.summary: Opens a card selection screen where a specific number of cards must be selected from an array of cards and returns the selection result. The cards will be displayed with an icon denoting which card pile they currently reside in.
member.param: The cards to select from
Shown cards will be sorted by pile type in the order they appear in this array.
member.returns: The selected cards

summary.para: The string format to use for the slider's label. Uses standard C# format, see .

If a localization entry YOURMOD-PROPERTY_NAME.sliderFormat exists in settings_ui, it takes precedence; this value is then only the fallback for languages without such an entry. Avoid putting words in here. Put them in the localization files instead.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Config.ConfigHoverTipAttribute
member.summary:
Show a tooltip for this setting on hover. Requires a settings_ui.json localization file. The names used are typically: YOURMOD-PROPERTY_NAME.hover.title (optional, if missing, no title will be shown)
YOURMOD-PROPERTY_NAME.hover.desc (required)
member.param: Enable hover tip for this property. Can be set to false in order to add exceptions for

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.ConfigHoverTipAttribute.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Show a tooltip for this setting on hover. Requires a settings_ui.json localization file. The names used are typically: YOURMOD-PROPERTY_NAME.hover.title (optional, if missing, no title will be shown)
YOURMOD-PROPERTY_NAME.hover.desc (required)
member.param: Enable hover tip for this property. Can be set to false in order to add exceptions for

member.name: T:BaseLib.Config.ConfigHoverTipsByDefaultAttribute
member.summary: Attempt to add hover tips to ALL config properties in this class. Use with enabled=false to add exceptions. Still requires .hover.desc entries in your localization file, see .

member.name: T:BaseLib.Config.ConfigIgnoreAttribute
member.summary:
Completely ignores this property. It will not be loaded or saved, and will not be shown for auto-generated UI.
Intended for properties that aren't configuration options at all.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Config.ConfigHideInUI
member.summary:
Saves and loads this property to the config file normally, but generates no UI for it in SimpleModConfig.
Intended for when you want to create the UI manually, or easily persist things that aren't user-configurable (e.g. total gold gained / number of runs played with the mod active).

member.name: T:BaseLib.Config.ConfigIgnoreRestoreDefaultsAttribute
member.summary:
Makes this property ignore Restore Defaults (whether via the button or via ).
Useful if you're storing some kind of persistent state, rather than a user configurable value.
Works with and without [].

member.name: T:BaseLib.Config.TextInputPreset
member.summary: Defines a set of allowed characters for a LineEdit.

member.name: F:BaseLib.Config.TextInputPreset.Anything
member.summary: The default. No validation at all.

member.name: F:BaseLib.Config.TextInputPreset.Alphanumeric
member.summary: English letters and numbers (a-z, A-Z, 0-9).

member.name: F:BaseLib.Config.TextInputPreset.AlphanumericWithSpaces
member.summary: English letters, numbers (a-z, A-Z, 0-9) and spaces.

member.name: F:BaseLib.Config.TextInputPreset.SafeDisplayName
member.summary: Allows international letters, numbers, spaces, underscores, and hyphens.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Config.ConfigTextInputAttribute
member.summary:
Defines the allowed characters and max length for the LineEdit generated by a string property.
You can specify a or a custom Regex string for the allowed characters. By default, any string is allowed, at any length. If a placeholder LocString exists (YOURMOD-PROPERTY_NAME.placeholder), it will be used; if you don't want a placeholder, simply skip creating such a string.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Config.ConfigButtonAttribute
member.summary:
Creates a clickable button from a method. Source code order is respected, so if the method is defined in between two properties, the button will be in between their respective controls.
The method supports having any of these different arguments injected with any name, in any order (only the type is used):
ModConfig: Injects the instance of your ModConfig (may be useful if the method is static).
NConfigButton: Injects the instance of the button that was clicked.
NConfigOptionRow: Injects the instance of the row the button belongs to (the node that handles its layout, hover tip, etc.).
member.param: LocString key for the text on the button.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.ConfigButtonAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary:
Creates a clickable button from a method. Source code order is respected, so if the method is defined in between two properties, the button will be in between their respective controls.
The method supports having any of these different arguments injected with any name, in any order (only the type is used):
ModConfig: Injects the instance of your ModConfig (may be useful if the method is static).
NConfigButton: Injects the instance of the button that was clicked.
NConfigOptionRow: Injects the instance of the row the button belongs to (the node that handles its layout, hover tip, etc.).
member.param: LocString key for the text on the button.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Config.ConfigButtonAttribute.Color
member.summary: The color to use for the button, as an HTML color code.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Config.ConfigVisibleIfAttribute

summary.para: Sets up conditional visibility for the marked property (or [ConfigButton] method). You can specify either a property or a method as a target, and optionally specify Invert = true to hide the row if the condition is true.

If a property is specified, its value will be compared to the arguments passed to this attribute; if the property value is equal to ANY argument, the row will be visible.
For a boolean property, you can leave the arguments out, in which case the row will be visible if the property is equal to true.

If you instead specify a method returning bool, the method will be executed, and its return value used to decide the row's visibility.
All arguments specified for the attribute will be injected to the method; in addition, you can specify arguments of types , (when annotating a property), (when annotating a method) and (to support both with a single method) to inject information about the current row.
You can combine these as you wish, e.g. bool MyMethod(PropertyInfo targetProp, int min, int max), and then specify two integer values in the attribute.

member.param: The property to compare against, or method to call. Use nameof(PropOrMethodName).
Optional arguments: see summary and examples.

example.b: Targeting a boolean property (argument optional, defaults to true):
Targeting a property with multiple allowed arguments:
Targeting a method with PropertyInfo auto-injection:
Targeting a method with explicitly injected arguments:

summary.para: Sets up conditional visibility for the marked property (or [ConfigButton] method). You can specify either a property or a method as a target, and optionally specify Invert = true to hide the row if the condition is true.

If a property is specified, its value will be compared to the arguments passed to this attribute; if the property value is equal to ANY argument, the row will be visible.
For a boolean property, you can leave the arguments out, in which case the row will be visible if the property is equal to true.

If you instead specify a method returning bool, the method will be executed, and its return value used to decide the row's visibility.
All arguments specified for the attribute will be injected to the method; in addition, you can specify arguments of types , (when annotating a property), (when annotating a method) and (to support both with a single method) to inject information about the current row.
You can combine these as you wish, e.g. bool MyMethod(PropertyInfo targetProp, int min, int max), and then specify two integer values in the attribute.

member.param: The property to compare against, or method to call. Use nameof(PropOrMethodName).
Optional arguments: see summary and examples.

example.b: Targeting a boolean property (argument optional, defaults to true):

member.name: P:BaseLib.Config.ConfigVisibleIfAttribute.Invert
member.summary: If true, the visibility condition is inverted. (e.g., Hidden when the method returns true, or hidden when the property matches).

member.name: T:BaseLib.Config.ConfigVisibleWhenAttribute
member.summary: No longer functional. Use ConfigVisibleIfAttribute instead.
member.param: The name of the property to watch (must be in the same ModConfig class).
The value the watched property must have for this row to be visible.
If true, the row is visible when the value does NOT match.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.ConfigVisibleWhenAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Object,System.Boolean)
member.summary: No longer functional. Use ConfigVisibleIfAttribute instead.
member.param: The name of the property to watch (must be in the same ModConfig class).
The value the watched property must have for this row to be visible.
If true, the row is visible when the value does NOT match.

summary.para:
Displays this property as a color picker element, and sets some properties on the color picker.
Not required for properties, if you want the default values.

Supported property types: and (HTML color code).
However, note that string is limited to standard 8-bit values, while Color supports a wider range if is true.
Beware: the default value for has alpha 0, so you likely want to set a default value, especially if EditAlpha is false.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Config.ConfigColorPickerAttribute.EditAlpha
member.summary: If true, allow the user to adjust the alpha (transparency) channel in the color picker popup. Defaults to true.

summary.para:
If true, allow the user to adjust the intensity slider, to support "overbright" or HDR-ish colors.
Disabled by default, since these colors are not supported for all use cases.
Note: This is ignored unless the property type is as these above-maximum values cannot be stored as hex strings.
Overrides the default localization key based on property name for dropdown elements.

Apply this attribute to multiple dropdown properties that should share the same localization entries. When specified, the provided override value is used instead of the property name when generating localization keys.

For example, if two dropdowns CardOption1 and CardOption2 use an override value of CARD_OPTIONS, a dropdown enum value BladeOfInk will use the key {modName}-CARD_OPTIONS.BladeOfInk for both properties.
Overrides the default localization key based on property name for dropdown elements.

Apply this attribute to multiple dropdown properties that should share the same localization entries. When specified, the provided override value is used instead of the property name when generating localization keys.

For example, if two dropdowns CardOption1 and CardOption2 use an override value of CARD_OPTIONS, a dropdown enum value BladeOfInk will use the key {modName}-CARD_OPTIONS.BladeOfInk for both properties.

member.name: E:BaseLib.Config.ModConfig.ConfigChanged
member.summary: Event that fires when is called. Custom controls must call Changed() when mutating a property.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.ModConfig.ModConfigLogger.Warn(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Show a message in the console, and optionally in the GUI. Only use showInGui=true if truly necessary; players won't enjoy having warnings/errors shoved in their faces unless it's something that truly impacts them.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.ModConfig.VisibleInModList
member.summary: If true, this ModConfig will show up in the mod list. By default, a mod is shown if it has any visible setting or button, but for a fully custom UI, there may be rare cases where you need to override this.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.ModConfig.RestoreDefaultsNoConfirm
member.summary: Restores the default value for all relevant config properties: all except those marked with [ConfigIgnore] or [ConfigIgnoreRestoreDefaults].

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.ModConfig.Load``1
member.summary: Static helper to reload your mod's config, for when you don't have easy access to the instance.

summary.para: Save after ms, unless SaveDebounced was called again during the delay, in which case the old request is canceled and the new one replaces it.
Note that you ONLY ever need to call this if you yourself modify the properties at runtime. BaseLib will always save when the user changes something in the settings screen.
Save after ms, unless SaveDebounced was called again during the delay, in which case the old request is canceled and the new one replaces it.
Note that you ONLY ever need to call this if you yourself modify the properties at runtime. BaseLib will always save when the user changes something in the settings screen.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.ModConfig.Save
member.summary:
Immediately save the current configuration to disk. Prefer using (on the instance or its static variant) instead unless you absolutely must save *now*. Indeed, using SaveDebounced(0) is likely still better than calling this directly.
Using both is not recommended and may cause issues with locking/hangs.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.ModConfig.CreateRawTickboxControl(System.Reflection.PropertyInfo)
member.summary:
Creates a raw control, with no layout (label, margins), no automatic hover tip, etc.
Use the Create*Option methods instead unless you need a custom layout (or use them, and customize them).
member.param: The property this control is bound to. Fetch with e.g. GetType().GetProperty() in a ModConfig.

member.summary: Creates a raw button control. You may want instead.
member.param: The text to place on the button
Action to perform when the user clicks/presses the button.

[^a-zA-Z0-9_.]

member.summary: Auto-generate a UI from the properties used. Should be enough for the vast majority of mods, but you can also subclass SimpleModConfig and override this to get access to helpers like (in addition to the raw Create*Control methods from ModConfig), without an auto-generated UI.

member.summary: Creates a layout-ready, non-collapsible section header row.

member.summary: See .

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.SimpleModConfig.CreateCollapsibleSection(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a collapsible section (header + content container). Use section.ContentContainer.AddChild() to add your content inside the collapsible container.
member.param: LocString for the label text.
If true, aligns the header text towards the top, rather than centered in the margins.
If true, the section starts out collapsed.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.SimpleModConfig.CreateRawHeaderLabel(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates the actual label used for a simple header or a collapsible header.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.SimpleModConfig.CreateStandardOption(System.Func{System.Reflection.PropertyInfo,Godot.Control},System.Reflection.PropertyInfo,System.Boolean)

summary.para: Creates a standard configuration row containing a label and an option control. It has default margins and optionally a hover tip (see for requirements).
You likely only need to call this if you create a custom control and want to use the default font/margin settings for it.

member.param: The property this option represents.
If true, automatically attaches a localized tooltip.
member.returns: A fully configured , ready to insert with AddChild.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.SimpleModConfig.GenerateOptionFromProperty(System.Reflection.PropertyInfo)
member.summary:
Auto-generates a UI row from a property, including a hover tip if [ConfigHoverTip] is specified.
Properties with [ConfigHideInUI] will NOT be ignored, so you can use this to manually create them if you wish.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.SimpleModConfig.GenerateButtonRowFromMethod(System.Reflection.MethodInfo)
member.summary:
Auto-generates a button row from a method marked with [ConfigButton], including a hover tip if [ConfigHoverTip] is specified.
Methods with [ConfigHideInUI] will NOT be ignored, so you can use this to manually create buttons for them if you wish.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.SimpleModConfig.GenerateOptionsForAllProperties(Godot.Control)
member.summary:
Auto-generate option rows for all properties in this SimpleModConfig. Runs by default, so that a subclass only needs to add its config properties, and nothing more, to get a reasonable UI. Properties marked with [ConfigHideInUI] will be ignored. Properties marked with [ConfigIgnore] won't even make it to this method.
Methods marked with [ConfigButton] will generate buttons.
member.param: Container where the generated options are inserted.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.SimpleModConfig.SetupFocusNeighbors(Godot.Control)
member.summary:
Connects the first focusable control on each row (each optionContainer child) to each other, for controller (and keyboard) navigation.
You can run this if you've added or modified controls in some way, to ensure navigation doesn't skip over controls, which can happen when Godot tries to guess which control is "next" or "previous" when you navigate.
member.param: The optionContainer passed to your SetupConfigUI method

members.member: Resolves arguments for [ConfigVisibleIf] target methods. Auto-injects some types when requested, and injects all arguments given to the attribute.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.UI.NConfigButton.SetColor(Godot.Color)
member.summary:
Sets the button's color using Godot's SelfModulate property.
The overall color will be slightly darker than the color specified, since it's a modulation of the existing colors, that aren't fully white.
member.param: The color to use for SelfModulate.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.UI.NConfigOptionRow.AddCustomHoverTip(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a HoverTip based on custom LocStrings. See to use LocStrings based on the setting's property name.
member.param: The LocString entry to use for the title (can be null)
The LocString entry to use for the description

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.UI.NConfigOptionRow.AddHoverTip
member.summary:
Adds a HoverTip to this row, using predefined entries in the localization file.
Localization is required; the game's HoverTip class doesn't let you use fixed strings. The names used are typically: YOURMOD-PROPERTY_NAME.hover.title (optional, if missing, no title will be shown)
YOURMOD-PROPERTY_NAME.hover.desc (required)

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.UI.NConfigOptionRow.RemoveHoverTip
member.summary: Removes a hover tip, if present.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.UI.NConfigSlider.SetRange(System.Double,System.Double,System.Nullable{System.Double})
member.summary:
Updates the slider's limits (and optionally step) atomically, ensuring no issues where e.g. min > max occur, even briefly.
The current value is clamped to fit inside [min, max].
Use instead if your values are known at compile time.
member.exception: If min is greater than or equal to max

member.name: M:BaseLib.Config.UI.NConfigSlider.ResolveDisplayFormat(BaseLib.Config.ModConfig,System.Reflection.PropertyInfo,System.String)
member.summary: Resolves the slider label format from localization: looks up {MODPREFIX}PROPERTY_NAME.sliderFormat in the settings_ui table, falling back to the attribute-supplied format when no entry exists.

member.summary: Grows outward from the empty side (e.g. doom-style).

member.name: T:BaseLib.Hooks.HealthBarForecastSegment
member.summary: One forecast overlay segment for a creature health bar.
member.param: HP amount represented by this segment.
Lethal HP label theming; also used as the forecast nine-patch when is null.
Which edge the segment grows from.
Lower values are rendered earlier in the chain. For , earlier segments stay closer to the current HP edge; for , earlier segments stay closer to the empty edge.
Optional Godot material (e.g. shader like vanilla doom). When null, only tint applies.
Optional for the forecast nine-patch. When null, is used for both overlay tint and lethal HP label; when set, is still used for lethal label theming.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Patches.Utils.LogListener.LogPrefixRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
^(?:\\[(?<level>VERYDEBUG|LOAD|DEBUG|INFO|WARN|ERROR)\\]|(?<level>VERYDEBUG|LOAD|DEBUG|INFO|WARN(?:ING)?|ERROR)\\b:?)\\s*(?<msg>.*)
Options:
RegexOptions.IgnoreCase | RegexOptions.Singleline
Explanation:
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match '['.
○ "level" capture group.
○ Match with 6 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match an empty string.
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Yy].
○ Match a character in the set [Dd].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Bb].
○ Match a character in the set [Uu].
○ Match a character in the set [Gg].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match an empty string.
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Dd].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match an empty string.
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Bb].
○ Match a character in the set [Uu].
○ Match a character in the set [Gg].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match an empty string.
○ Match a character in the set [Nn].
○ Match a character in the set [Ff].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match an empty string.
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Nn].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match an empty string.
○ Match a character in the set [Rr] exactly 2 times.
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match ']'.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ "level" capture group.
○ Match with 6 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Vv].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Yy].
○ Match a character in the set [Dd].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Bb].
○ Match a character in the set [Uu].
○ Match a character in the set [Gg].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Ll].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Dd].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Dd].
○ Match a character in the set [Ee].
○ Match a character in the set [Bb].
○ Match a character in the set [Uu].
○ Match a character in the set [Gg].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Ii].
○ Match a character in the set [Nn].
○ Match a character in the set [Ff].
○ Match a character in the set [Oo].
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character in the set [Ww].
○ Match a character in the set [Aa].
○ Match a character in the set [Rr].
○ Match a character in the set [Nn].
○ Optional (greedy).

member.name: T:BaseLib.Patches.Features.AutoPlayCustomTargetPatch
member.summary:
only randomizes a target for the vanilla and types, so a card with a custom single-target type that is auto-played without a target ends up played with a null target and throws. This fills in the missing fallback by picking a random valid target using the registered predicate.
Fully defensive: it never blocks the original method, and any problem leaves the target untouched so the vanilla path runs exactly as before.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Patches.Features.BetterConsoleAutocompletePatch
member.summary: Improves dev console autocomplete to use instead of when no custom predicate is set, allowing partial matches anywhere in the candidate name (e.g. "STRI" matches "MYMOD-STRIKE_MYCHARACTER"). Results are sorted so direct prefix matches appear first, followed by tag-suffix matches, then all others.

member.summary: Sorts candidates so direct prefix matches come first, mod-tag-suffix matches second, and all other contains-matches last.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Patches.Features.UpdateGhostTextPatch
member.summary: Suppresses the call in that fires when CommonPrefix does not start with the current input text. This is no longer an error with contains-based autocomplete.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Patches.Localization.SimpleLoc.GoldHighlightRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
(?<=^|[^/])\\*({.+?}|.+?(?=$|[\\s*.,|}]))\\*?
Explanation:
○ Zero-width positive lookbehind.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions, atomically.
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match any character other than '/' right-to-left.
○ Match '*'.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match '{'.
○ Match a character other than '\n' lazily at least once.
○ Match '}'.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character other than '\n' lazily at least once.
○ Zero-width positive lookahead.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions, atomically.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.
○ Match a character in the set [*,.|}\s].
○ Match '*' atomically, optionally.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Patches.Localization.SimpleLoc.BlueHighlightRegex
member.remarks:

(?<=^|[^/])\\$({.+?}|.+?(?=$|[\\s$.,|}]))\\$?
Explanation:
○ Zero-width positive lookbehind.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions, atomically.
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match any character other than '/' right-to-left.
○ Match '$'.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match '{'.
○ Match a character other than '\n' lazily at least once.
○ Match '}'.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match a character other than '\n' lazily at least once.
○ Zero-width positive lookahead.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions, atomically.
○ Match if at the end of the string or if before an ending newline.
○ Match a character in the set [$,.|}\s].
○ Match '$' atomically, optionally.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Patches.Localization.SimpleLoc.NormalVariableRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
({)([^:}.]+)([:}])
Explanation:
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match '{'.
○ 2nd capture group.
○ Match a character in the set [^.:}] greedily at least once.
○ 3rd capture group.
○ Match a character in the set [:}].

member.name: P:BaseLib.Patches.Localization.SimpleLoc.DiffVariableRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
!(.*?)!
Explanation:
○ Match '!'.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a character other than '\n' lazily any number of times.
○ Match '!'.

○ 1st capture group.

Explanation:
○ Zero-width positive lookbehind.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions, atomically.
○ Match if at the beginning of the string.
○ Match any character other than '/' right-to-left.
○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match '-'.
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a character other than '\n' lazily at least once.
○ Match '-'.
○ Match a sequence of expressions.
○ Match '+'.
○ 2nd capture group.
○ Match a character other than '\n' lazily any number of times.
○ Match any character other than '/'.
○ Match '+'.

○ Match a character other than '\n' lazily any number of times.

○ Match '+'.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Patches.Localization.SimpleLoc.PluralizeRegex
member.remarks:
Pattern:
(.*?{)([^{]+?)((?::[^{]*)?}(?:(?:[^{]*?[^{/])|(?:)))\\(([^()]+?)\\)
Explanation:
○ 1st capture group.
○ Match a character other than '\n' lazily any number of times.
○ Match '{'.
○ 2nd capture group.
○ Match a character other than '{' lazily at least once.
○ 3rd capture group.
○ Optional (greedy).

member.name: T:BaseLib.Patches.UI.SceneConversionPatch
member.summary:
Patches the non-generic PackedScene.Instantiate so that registered scenes are auto-converted to the correct node type before Instantiate<T>'s castclass runs.
This makes it so modders can use standard Godot scenes (Node2D root, etc) and have them transparently converted to game types like NCreatureVisuals without needing per-callsite Harmony patches.
Uses TargetMethod to avoid ambiguity between the generic and non-generic overloads.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Patches.UI.RegisterSceneConversions
member.summary: Registers custom scene paths. Called through a patch because virtual properties like CustomVisualPath may depend on fields set in derived constructors that haven't run yet when the base constructor occurs.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Utils.AncientOption`1.ModelPrep
member.summary: Set this if relic needs to set up data based on current run state, eg. Sea Glass choosing a random other character. This will be set up per-event instance.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Utils.CustomBackgroundAssets.#ctor(System.String,MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Random.Rng)
member.summary: Loads a set of assets from a provided directory for layers and path to a background scene.
member.param: Path to a directory containing assets with names containing _fg_ or _bg_ and ending with a number, each of which should be an individual scene. _fg_ scenes can be named in any way. _bg_ assets should be split into numbered layers such as myact_bg_00_a.tscn, myact_bg_01_a.tscn, myact_bg_01_b.tscn being two numbered sets, 00 and 01. A single random foreground scene will be chosen, and a random asset from each numbered layer will be cho sen from the background scenes. These scenes are generally about 2900x1350, with a centered anchor. size (2881.54x1350.72), position (-480.77, -135.36), root TextureRect expand mode IgnoreSize Anchors preset Center. Size is not "fixed". Can be wider. Actual texture of texturerect is usually 2048x960.
The rng passed to a method where this is called. In ActModel, GenerateBackgroundAssets.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Utils.CustomBackgroundAssets.#ctor(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.String},System.String)
member.summary: Use a fixed set of assets as background assets. All paths should be .tscn files.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Utils.CustomBackgroundAssets.#ctor(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String}},System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},MegaCrit.Sts2.Core.Random.Rng)
member.summary: Use a random subset of a fixed set of assets as background assets. All paths should be .tscn files.
member.param: Set of layers; one random item will be taken from each layer.
One random .tscn will be used for the foreground layer.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Utils.FmodAudio
M:BaseLib.Utils.FmodAudio.PlayEvent(System.String)

member.summary: Play an FMOD event with cooldown. If the same event was played within cooldownMs, the call is silently skipped. Useful for sounds triggered by rapid game events (e.g. a power that fires on every card play).

member.name: M:BaseLib.Utils.FmodAudio.PlayEventByGuid(System.String)
member.summary: Play an event by its GUID instead of path. Slightly faster lookup and immune to event renames in FMOD Studio.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Utils.FmodAudio.CreateEventInstance(System.String)
member.summary: Create an event instance you can start/stop/adjust yourself. Don't forget to call Release() on the returned object when you're done.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Utils.FmodAudio.PlayFile(System.String,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary:
Play a sound file (wav, ogg, mp3) through FMOD. The file gets loaded into FMOD's memory on first use and stays cached for subsequent calls.
Returns the FmodSound handle so you can control it (stop, volume, pitch). The handle has: play(), stop(), set_volume(float), set_pitch(float), is_playing(), set_paused(bool), release().

member.name: M:BaseLib.Utils.FmodAudio.PlayFile(System.String,System.Int32,System.Single,System.Single)

member.name: M:BaseLib.Utils.FmodAudio.PreloadFile(System.String)
member.summary: Load a sound file into FMOD without playing it. Useful if you want to preload during init so there's no hitch on first play.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Utils.FmodAudio.PreloadMusic(System.String)
member.summary: Load a music/long-form file as streaming (reads from disk during playback instead of loading the whole thing into memory). Use this for tracks longer than ~10 seconds to save RAM.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Utils.FmodAudio.PlayMusic(System.String,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Play a long-form audio file using streaming (doesn't load entire file into memory). Ideal for custom music tracks, long ambient loops, etc.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Utils.FmodAudio.CreateSoundInstance(System.String)

member.summary: Load a custom FMOD bank. Must be built with FMOD Studio 2.03.x to match the game's runtime. Load your strings bank first, then content banks.

Register a replacement for a game sound. When the game tries to play originalEvent, your replacement runs instead. Lets multiple mods swap sounds without writing their own Harmony patches.
The handler receives (eventPath, volume) and returns true if it handled playback (which skips the original), or false to let it through.

Checks if the instantiated node needs auto-conversion based on registered scene types, and performs the conversion if a matching factory exists. Called from the PackedScene.Instantiate postfix.
When CreateFromScene also calls Instantiate internally, the postfix handles the conversion and CreateFromScene's "if (n is T t) return t" short-circuits — both paths produce the same result.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Utils.Patching.AsyncMethodCall
member.summary: Utility class for patching async method state machines. Can generate additional states to call an async method.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Utils.Patching.AsyncMethodCall.Create(System.Reflection.Emit.ILGenerator,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{HarmonyLib.CodeInstruction},System.Reflection.MethodBase,System.Reflection.MethodInfo,System.Reflection.MethodBase,System.Reflection.MethodBase,System.String)
member.summary:
Given the CodeInstructions of an async state machine's MoveNext method, insert an async method call into it, creating another state. beforeState or afterState must be provided, being an async method awaited by the original method (and so having its own state). The original method itself can be passed to beforeState or afterState. If so, the target position will be the first or last state.
Parameters of the method will attempt to be found by name. If a parameter cannot be determined an exception will be thrown.
If resultName is provided and the method to call returns a value, it will be stored in a variable of this name using an external dictionary, and can be passed into subsequent calls by defining a parameter with this name.
If resultName is "return" the result of the method will attempt to be returned immediately. If resultName is "returnIf" and the method called has a boolean return value, the state machine will return early. This will not work if the state machine method has a non-void return type.
If resultName is the same as one of the method's parameters, it will be attempted to store the result in the variable passed to that parameter. If this does not match the correct return type, an exception will be thrown when patching.
member.param: The method being patched, provided to transpiler patches.
A method that returns a Task that will be called.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Utils.Patching.AsyncMethodSections.BranchingStateSection.BranchTo(BaseLib.Utils.Patching.AsyncMethodSections.AsyncMethodContext,BaseLib.Utils.Patching.AsyncMethodSections.StateInfo,System.Reflection.Emit.ILGenerator,System.Reflection.Emit.Label@)
member.summary: Generates code instructions to branch to a new state labeled with resumeLabel.

member.summary: Iterates over given matchers and attempts to match each in order. After matching is complete, position is on the code instruction following the last match. If a match is not found, onFailMatch is called. By default, this will throw an exception.

member.summary: Iterates over given matchers and attempts to match each in order. After matching is complete, position is on the code instruction following the last match. If a match is not found, null will be returned, skipping operations chained with the ?. operator.

member.summary: Iterates over given matchers and attempts to match each in order, starting from the end of the method. After matching is complete, position is on the code instruction following the last match. If a match is not found, an exception will be thrown.

member.name: T:BaseLib.Utils.SpireField`2
member.summary: A basic wrapper around for convenience. While this can be used to store value types, they will be boxed and thus is somewhat inefficient.

member.name: M:BaseLib.Utils.SpireField`2.CopyOnClone(System.Action{`0,`0,`1})
member.summary: Causes this SpireField's value to be copied when the model it is attached to is cloned. Only valid for SpireFields attached to types inheriting from AbstractModel. The value will only be copied over if it has been set at least once outside of the default value, or if the default value is a reference type and its value has been retrieved at least once. Note that this is a shallow clone; reference types will be assigned directly to the new instance, not copied. Optional cloneVal parameter will change how the value is copied to the new instance.
member.param: A function to copy the value to the new model. Receives the source model, destination model, and the field's value for the source model. Should call Set on this SpireField with the destination model or otherwise copy values over.

member.name: P:BaseLib.Utils.SpireField`2.ShouldClone
member.summary: Returns true if this SpireField's value should be copied over when a model it is attached to is cloned.
